LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ERICK LYLE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: DENY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH CCP 437(C)B)(1) REQUIREMENT OF SEPARATE STATEMENT. ADDITIONALLY, THE COURT RULED IN THE ALTERNATIVE THAT THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED ON ITS MERITS AND THAT THERE EXISTS TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PAUL H. ALVARADO, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR 5797

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Mar-16-2009 Dismissal - No Appearance

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, UNLAWFUL DETAINER
Off Calendar Jan-26-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT ERICK LYLE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
Appearances: Stanley W. Riddell, Esq. of Sedgwick, (415) 627-3610, appearing for plaintiff and opposing party; John Viola, Esq.,, Attorney at Law, (415) 864-4230, appearing for Defendant Eric Lyle and moving party. Hearing held and argued. Ruling: The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DENY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH CCP 437(c)b)(1) REQUIREMENT OF SEPARATE STATEMENT. Additionally, the Court ruled in the alternative that the Motion for summary judgment is denied on its merits and that there exists triable issues of fact. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/PHA/JW) Judge: Paul H. Alvarado, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5797

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jan-26-2009 Continued To Mar-16-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 206
